content.problemStatement:
**IIT Ropar** is hosting its tech fest, Advitiya, on the 16\textbf{16}16-th, 17\textbf{17}17-th, and 18\textbf{18}18-th of February.

Mehul, looking for a vacation, decides to visit Ropar in the month of February.

Mehul learned of Advitiya, and found out that there are no registration fees — even accommodation is being provided to the participants for free!

Team Advitiya is very welcoming, so Mehul definitely wants to attend the fest.

Mehul will visit Ropar on date NNN (which is between 111 and 181818) of February. Will he be able to enjoy the fest?

Print `"ADVITIYA"` if NNN is one of the days on which Advitiya is running, and `"WAITING FOR ADVITIYA"` otherwise.


Input Format
The first line contains a single integer NNN, the date on which Mehul decided to visit Ropar.



Output Format
Print a single line containing the answer: `"ADVITIYA"` if Mehul visits on the right date, and `"WAITING FOR ADVITIYA"` otherwise.

Both strings are to be printed without the quotes.


Each character of the output may be printed in either uppercase or lowercase, i.e, the strings `ADVITIYA`, `advitiya`, and `AdViTiYa` will all be treated as equivalent.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single integer N, the date (1 ≤ N ≤ 31) on which Mehul will visit Ropor.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a line containing either "ADVITIYA" if N is 16, 17 or 18, otherwise output "WAITING FOR ADVITIYA". The case of letters does not matter.

sampleTestCases.explanation: N=5 is not one of the fest dates (16,17,18), so Mehul must wait.
N=17 matches a fest day, therefore Mehul can attend.
First date 15 is outside the range, second 16 and third 18 are fest days.

hidden.label: general case 1
hidden.expectedOutput:
WAITING FOR ADVITIYA
ADVITIYA
WAITING FOR ADVITIYA
ADVITIYA

hidden.label: general case 2
hidden.expectedOutput:
WAITING FOR ADVITIYA
WAITING FOR ADVITIYA

solution.approach: Read the number of test cases. For each test case read N and check whether N equals 16, 17 or 18. If it does, print "ADVITIYA"; otherwise print "WAITING FOR ADVITIYA". This is O(1) work per test case.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – constant work for each of the T test cases.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few integer variables are used.
